primary_legislation.england_wales: Environmental Protection Act 1990; Controlled Waste (England and Wales) Regulations 2012; Hazardous Waste Regulations 2005; HTM 07-01 (Safe Management of Healthcare Waste, England and Wales)
primary_legislation.scotland: Environmental Protection Act 1990; Special Waste Regulations 1996 (Scotland); HFS (Health Facilities Scotland) Healthcare Waste Management guidance aligned to HTM 07-01
primary_legislation.northern_ireland: Waste and Contaminated Land (NI) Order 1997; Hazardous Waste Regulations (NI) 2005; HSCB/PHA guidance on healthcare waste aligned to HTM 07-01

notes: GP practices generate multiple clinical waste streams that require segregation at the point of generation per HTM 07-01 (or equivalent). The key streams are: (1) Sharps/infectious waste — yellow-lidded sharps bins; (2) Infectious clinical waste — yellow-bagged (soft offensive clinical waste); (3) Offensive/hygiene waste — yellow/black tiger-striped bags (non-infectious, non-hazardous clinical waste); (4) Medicines waste — blue-lidded bins (non-cytotoxic) or purple-lidded bins (cytotoxic/cytostatic); (5) Amalgam waste (if any dental activity). General non-clinical waste is covered in GP-MOD-016.

logbooks.title: Daily Clinical Waste Segregation Check
logbooks.frequency_detail: At the end of each clinical session.

steps.description: Check all sharps bins in clinical rooms: confirm fill level does not exceed the fill line (maximum two-thirds full). When the fill line is reached, assemble the temporary closure mechanism, lock the bin, and replace with a new bin.

Check clinical waste bins in clinical rooms: confirm correct colour coding — yellow bags for infectious clinical waste; yellow/black tiger-striped bags for offensive/hygiene waste; blue-lidded bins for non-cytotoxic medicines waste; purple-lidded for cytotoxic waste (dispensing practices). Do not overfill bags beyond the swan-neck tie point.
Ensure no clinical waste has been placed in the domestic/municipal waste stream (black bags).
steps.photo_guidance: Photograph any overfilled sharps bin or incorrectly segregated waste.

steps.description: Move full clinical waste containers to the designated secure clinical waste storage area (lockable, separate from domestic waste, not accessible to patients or members of public).
Record: date, areas checked, any full bins replaced, any segregation errors found, action taken, name of checker.

logbooks.title: Monthly Clinical Waste Storage Area Inspection

steps.description: Inspect the clinical waste storage area: confirm it is locked, clearly labelled as clinical waste storage, not accessible to the public, and provides suitable containment (lidded/sealed containers) to prevent spillage.

Confirm waste is not accumulating beyond the permitted storage period: clinical waste must not be stored on-site for more than the period specified in the practice's waste management contract (typically maximum 7 days for infectious/hazardous clinical waste, longer may be permitted for offensive waste — confirm with contractor).
Confirm the collection schedule is on file and the waste contractor's licence is current (EA/NRW/SEPA/NIEA registered carrier, licensed to transport clinical waste).
Confirm the area is free from pest activity (no evidence of rodents or insects — cross-reference with GP-MOD-012 pest control records).
Record: date, storage area condition, waste accumulation within permitted period (yes/no), contractor licence confirmed, any issues found, action taken, inspector name.

logbooks.title: Annual Clinical Waste Contractor Audit and Consignment Record Review

steps.description: Review all clinical waste consignment notes / duty of care documentation for the past 12 months: confirm all collections have been documented, signed by the carrier, and copies retained on file (minimum 2 years for non-hazardous waste transfer notes, minimum 3 years for hazardous waste consignment notes).

Confirm the clinical waste contractor holds a current waste carrier licence appropriate for clinical waste (check EA/NRW/SEPA/NIEA registers). Confirm they are licensed to handle infectious/hazardous waste.

Confirm compliance with HTM 07-01 (Safe Management of Healthcare Waste) as applicable to primary care. Review whether the practice's waste streams are correctly classified per the current HTM 07-01 colour-coded segregation guide. EA enforces Hazardous Waste Regulations.
Confirm compliance with HTM 07-01 as adopted in Wales. NRW enforces waste regulations in Wales.

Confirm compliance with HFS healthcare waste management guidance (aligned to HTM 07-01). SEPA enforces Special Waste Regulations in Scotland. Note: in Scotland, medicines waste may be managed differently — confirm current SEPA/HFS guidance.
Confirm compliance with HSCB/PHA NI healthcare waste guidance. NIEA enforces waste regulations in NI.
Confirm staff training in clinical waste segregation is current for all clinical and dispensary staff.
Record: date, consignment notes reviewed, contractor licence confirmed, waste streams correctly classified, staff training confirmed, any gaps identified, action plan, reviewer name.
